title: Biometric Britain: The Expansion of Facial Recognition Surveillance
content_summary: Report documenting rapid growth in police use of live facial recognition. Found 86%+ inaccuracy rates in Met and South Wales Police deployments. Called for moratorium on LFR pending a statutory framework. Included briefing for DPDI Bill committee stage.
processing_activities: Biometric data processing; facial recognition; police surveillance; special category data
topic_tags: facial recognition; biometrics; police surveillance; LFR; DPDI Bill; inaccuracy; moratorium
target_org: Metropolitan Police; South Wales Police; Home Office

title: Complaint to ICO re welfare fraud-scoring algorithms
content_summary: Formal ICO complaint following Poverty Panopticon investigation. Found 540000 benefit applicants secretly assigned fraud-risk scores; 1.6 million people in social housing profiled by commercial algorithms. Argued processing lacked lawful basis and violated fairness and transparency principles.
processing_activities: Automated decision-making; welfare data processing; profiling; algorithmic scoring
topic_tags: ADM; welfare; algorithms; fraud scoring; profiling; local authorities; UK GDPR; ICO complaint
outcome: ICO acknowledged complaint; no enforcement action confirmed by end 2024

title: Briefing on DPDI Bill No.2 for House of Commons Committee Stage
content_summary: Argued DPDI Bill weakened data subject rights including rights against automated decisions; would undermine ICO independence; proposed amendments to restore UK GDPR protections including ADM safeguards. Filed alongside Liberty and ORG.
processing_activities: Automated decision-making; data subject rights; ICO independence; UK GDPR
topic_tags: DPDI Bill; ADM; data subject rights; ICO independence; UK GDPR reform; parliamentary briefing

title: Bossware: The Dangers of High Tech Worker Surveillance
content_summary: Report exposing use of AI-powered workplace monitoring tools by UK employers. Documented employee tracking through keystroke logging; screen capture; biometric attendance monitoring. Called for statutory ban on covert employee surveillance and ICO enforcement action.
processing_activities: Employment data processing; workplace surveillance; biometric data; employee monitoring
topic_tags: bossware; workplace surveillance; employee data; ICO enforcement; biometric attendance; AI monitoring

title: Police facial recognition searches of passport database legal challenge
content_summary: Announced judicial review against Home Office and police forces over secret use of facial recognition to search passport database. FOI data showed searches skyrocketed from 2 in 2020 to 417 in 2023. Argued unlawful mass biometric surveillance without statutory framework or public knowledge.
processing_activities: Biometric data processing; facial recognition; passport database; police surveillance
topic_tags: facial recognition; passport database; police; Home Office; judicial review; biometrics; mass surveillance
outcome: Legal challenge filed; ongoing at April 2026

title: Briefing on Data Use and Access Bill 2024
content_summary: Raised concerns that DUA Bill enabled automated fraud detection across DWP and HMRC data with insufficient safeguards. Called for amendments requiring human review of all automated decisions affecting benefits claimants and mandatory DPIAs for public sector AI tools.
processing_activities: Automated decision-making; welfare data; benefits processing; DPIA obligations
topic_tags: DUA Bill; ADM; welfare; DWP; HMRC; DPIA; human oversight; public sector AI

title: ORG complaint to ICO about LiveRamp adtech data processing
content_summary: Complaint to ICO and French CNIL against LiveRamp's RampID identity graph system. Found processing indiscriminately linked names and addresses with browsing histories for millions of UK users without adequate legal basis or transparency. Part of ongoing ORG campaign against unlawful adtech since 2018.
processing_activities: Behavioural advertising; data brokerage; profiling; consent; lawful basis
topic_tags: adtech; LiveRamp; RampID; consent; lawful basis; profiling; ICO complaint; CNIL; surveillance capitalism
outcome: ICO acknowledged; outcome pending at April 2026

title: ORG complaint to ICO re Meta AI training on UK user data
content_summary: Formal ICO complaint about Meta's use of Facebook and Instagram user data to train generative AI models without explicit consent. Argued no valid lawful basis; processing violated purpose limitation and data minimisation principles. ICO subsequently contacted Meta and Meta paused UK AI training.
processing_activities: Generative AI training; social media data; purpose limitation; consent; lawful basis
topic_tags: Meta; generative AI; AI training; lawful basis; purpose limitation; consent; ICO complaint; UK GDPR
outcome: Meta paused UK AI training in June 2024 following ICO intervention. ORG met with ICO in late 2024.

title: Alternative ICO Annual Report 2023-24
content_summary: Critical assessment of ICO enforcement record. Found ICO issued only 4 private sector data protection fines in 2023-24. Compared unfavourably with European DPAs. Criticised two-year public sector trial for limiting fines. Called for statutory duty to publish enforcement logic; benchmarking against EU peers; mandatory priority sector lists.
processing_activities: ICO enforcement; data protection fines; public sector enforcement
topic_tags: ICO enforcement; alternative annual report; private sector fines; public sector trial; ORG; accountability

title: Briefing on DPDI Bill HoL Committee Stage on ICO statutory duty
content_summary: Amendment briefing for Lords Committee Stage. Proposed statutory requirement for ICO to publish assessment logic for all enforcement decisions including non-investigation decisions. Sought to remove secondary objectives framing innovation and growth as competing with data protection enforcement.
processing_activities: ICO enforcement; data subject rights; secondary objectives; ICO independence
topic_tags: DPDI Bill; ICO statutory duty; enforcement transparency; secondary objectives; ORG; Lords

title: Joint briefing with BBW and Index on Censorship urging OSA reform or repeal
content_summary: Joint briefing to MPs ahead of parliamentary debate on petition to repeal the Online Safety Act signed by 550000+ people. Argued OSA threatens user privacy through algorithmic surveillance; restricts free expression; creates discrimination risk through age verification face checks; blocks millions without ID from internet access.
processing_activities: Online content moderation; age verification; biometric data; user profiling
topic_tags: Online Safety Act; OSA repeal; age verification; facial recognition; free expression; privacy; ORG; BBW

title: Research and advocacy on Palantir NHS data contracts
content_summary: Ongoing research mapping Palantir's connections with UK state including NHS Federated Data Platform contract. Raised data protection concerns about lack of transparency in contract terms; data subject rights; international transfers; lawful basis for sensitive health data processing by a US defence contractor.
processing_activities: Health data processing; international transfers; lawful basis; data subject rights; special category data
topic_tags: Palantir; NHS; health data; international transfers; surveillance capitalism; ICO; data subject rights

title: Written evidence to Science and Technology Committee inquiry on AI governance
content_summary: Submitted evidence on need for binding AI regulation with strong data protection integration. Argued pro-innovation AI white paper insufficient; called for mandatory DPIAs for all high-risk AI; transparency requirements for training data; extraterritorial enforcement for UK resident data.
processing_activities: AI processing; DPIA obligations; training data transparency; extraterritorial enforcement
topic_tags: AI governance; DPIA; training data; extraterritorial; binding regulation; human rights; Privacy International

title: Home Office visa streaming tool legal challenge (settled 2023)
content_summary: Foxglove and JCWI legal challenge against Home Office algorithmic visa streaming tool. Argued AI tool directing applications to fast/slow/full-scrutiny lanes was discriminatory and lacked transparency. Home Office ultimately scrapped the tool and acknowledged its unlawful operation.
processing_activities: Automated decision-making; immigration data processing; algorithmic discrimination; special category data
topic_tags: Home Office; visa algorithm; ADM; discrimination; immigration; algorithmic transparency; Foxglove; JCWI
outcome: Home Office scrapped streaming tool; acknowledged issues. Settled 2023.

title: Foxglove and Global Action Plan legal challenge re hyperscale data centre approval
content_summary: Planning statutory review filed against Deputy Prime Minister's decision to approve 90MW hyperscale data centre at Iver without environmental impact assessment. Argued data centres' water and power consumption requires full EIA; government failed to assess impact on local infrastructure.
processing_activities: Data centre operations; environmental impact; AI infrastructure
topic_tags: data centres; hyperscale; AI infrastructure; planning; environmental; Foxglove; judicial review

title: GMCDP and Foxglove challenge to DWP fraud detection algorithm
content_summary: Judicial review challenge against DWP's use of automated fraud detection algorithms affecting disabled benefit claimants. Argued processing lacked lawful basis; violated rights under UK GDPR and Equality Act 2010; no meaningful human review of automated decisions adversely affecting claimants.
processing_activities: Automated decision-making; welfare data; algorithmic discrimination; disability data
topic_tags: DWP; fraud detection; ADM; disabled claimants; UK GDPR; Equality Act; judicial review; Foxglove

title: Liberty critique of facial recognition at Beyonce Cardiff concert and King's Coronation
content_summary: Published legal analysis criticising police use of LFR at major public events without adequate legal framework. Argued processing disproportionate; reinforced discriminatory policing; violated Art.8 ECHR privacy rights. Called for legislative framework before any further police LFR deployments.
processing_activities: Biometric data processing; facial recognition; public event surveillance; special category data
topic_tags: Liberty; facial recognition; LFR; police; Cardiff; King's Coronation; Art.8 ECHR; proportionality

title: Liberty briefing on DUA Bill — ICO independence and secondary objectives
content_summary: Briefing opposing DUA Bill amendment removing ICO's obligation to prioritise data protection over secondary innovation and growth objectives. Argued amendment creates structural conflict of interest and risks EU adequacy. Supported Lord Clement-Jones amendment HoL122.
processing_activities: ICO independence; EU adequacy; data protection principles
topic_tags: DUA Bill; ICO independence; secondary objectives; EU adequacy; Liberty; Lord Clement-Jones; HoL122

title: Foxglove investigation into government closeness to US Big Tech AI companies
content_summary: Investigation into relationships between UK government ministers and US Big Tech AI companies including Anthropic and OpenAI. Raised concerns about procurement decisions; lack of transparency in GOV.UK Chat supplier selection; adequacy of data protection assessments for government AI contracts.
processing_activities: AI procurement; government data; lawful basis; international transfers
topic_tags: Foxglove; Big Tech; government AI; Anthropic; OpenAI; procurement; transparency; GOV.UK Chat

title: ORG briefing on OSA age verification and privacy implications
content_summary: Policy brief examining privacy risks of age assurance mechanisms required under Online Safety Act. Argued biometric age verification creates disproportionate processing of special category data; alternative methods such as device-based verification should be preferred. Submitted to Ofcom consultation.
processing_activities: Biometric data processing; age verification; special category data; children's data
topic_tags: OSA; age verification; biometrics; children's data; Ofcom; ICO; privacy; ORG
